purpose: Test whether a hand-written global execution card plus object-conditioned decision units has value before implementing dual-layer rendering.
conclusion: The decision units reduce invalid actions and slightly increase complete success, but the card is too conservative or causes extra exploration: reward and step efficiency are worse than the recorded NoSkill Test-A reference. This validates the behavioral target, not a positive end-to-end result. Use it as a renderer design probe and do not adopt it as a main method result.
